content_profile.learning_objective: Explain how DDPMs define a fixed forward noising process and learn a reverse denoising process over many timesteps.
content_profile.visual_thesis: The video shows a clean sample moving along a timestep chain into noise, then a learned denoiser repeatedly steps the noisy state back toward structure.
scope: Show x_0 as a clean sample on a data manifold abstraction.
Show beta_t as a fixed forward noise schedule.
Show alpha_bar_t as cumulative signal retention for direct sampling of x_t.
Show epsilon_theta guiding a gradual reverse process rather than a one-shot jump.

entities.label: x_0
entities.technical_role: Clean data sample at the start of the forward process.
entities.meaning: The structured source state before noise is added.
entities.visualization_need: Provides the before-state that viewers compare against noisy and reconstructed states.
entities.claim_boundary: Shown as a simple teaching tile, not a real training image.

entities.label: x_t
entities.technical_role: Latent state after adding noise at timestep t.
entities.meaning: The sample after partial or near-complete corruption by Gaussian noise.
entities.visualization_need: Needs a stable identity across forward and reverse chains.
entities.claim_boundary: Particle-cloud form is a low-dimensional teaching abstraction.

entities.label: beta_t
entities.technical_role: Fixed variance schedule for the forward noising steps.
entities.meaning: Controls how much Gaussian noise is injected over time.
entities.visualization_need: Must be visually separate from the learned reverse model.
entities.claim_boundary: The curve shape is illustrative unless a concrete schedule is chosen.

entities.label: alpha_bar_t
entities.technical_role: Cumulative signal retention used for direct sampling of x_t from x_0.
entities.meaning: Shows how clean signal fades as timestep increases.
entities.visualization_need: Links the direct closed-form expression to a visible slider.
entities.claim_boundary: Curve is schematic unless backed by a chosen beta schedule.

entities.label: epsilon_theta
entities.technical_role: Learned network used in the reverse denoising process.
entities.meaning: Predicts noise or guides the reverse transition.
entities.visualization_need: Must be marked as learned, unlike the forward schedule.
entities.claim_boundary: Rendered as a module, not a full U-Net architecture.

entities.label: x_{t-1}
entities.technical_role: Previous timestep sample produced by one learned reverse step.
entities.meaning: A slightly less noisy state after denoising.

operators.label: Forward noising
operators.technical_meaning: Sample a noisier latent state according to the fixed forward transition.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Diffuse the clean sample along a timestep chain while a beta_t dial increases.

operators.label: Closed-form x_t sampling
operators.technical_meaning: Sample x_t directly from x_0 using alpha_bar_t.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Move a slider on the signal-retention curve and reveal the corresponding noise level.

operators.label: Reverse denoising step
operators.technical_meaning: Use the learned reverse process to step from x_t toward x_{t-1}.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Route the noisy state through epsilon_theta and reduce cloud dispersion slightly.

operators.label: Reverse chain
operators.technical_meaning: Repeat reverse steps until structure emerges.
operators.visual_operation_hint: Iterate denoising across several timesteps with a visible countdown.

states.label: Clean sample
states.description: The sample begins as a structured clean tile.

states.label: Forward noised state
states.description: The sample becomes more noisy as the fixed forward schedule advances.

states.label: Direct x_t sample
states.description: A time slider reveals x_t sampled directly from x_0 using cumulative signal retention.

states.label: Reverse denoise
states.description: The learned denoiser guides repeated steps from noise toward structure.

relations.meaning: The forward schedule determines how noise grows with timestep.
alpha_bar_t controls the mix of retained signal and Gaussian noise.
The learned reverse step maps a noisy state to a slightly less noisy predecessor.

invariants.statement: The forward noising schedule is fixed, while the reverse denoiser is learned.
invariants.visual_test: beta_t appears as a fixed schedule; epsilon_theta appears as a learned module.

invariants.statement: The reverse process is shown as multiple denoising steps, not a one-step jump.
invariants.visual_test: Several timestep ticks remain visible during reverse convergence.

invariants.statement: Forward timesteps increase from x_0 to x_T; reverse timesteps decrease from x_T to x_0.
invariants.visual_test: Timeline arrow direction and labels remain consistent.

assumptions.statement: Noise clouds are low-dimensional teaching abstractions.
assumptions.impact_on_visualization: Particles can show dispersion, but narration must not imply exact pixel values.

assumptions.statement: The schedule and signal curve are schematic unless a toy beta schedule is supplied.
assumptions.impact_on_visualization: Do not make numeric schedule claims without explicit values.

spaces.coordinate_system: left-to-right forward time and right-to-left reverse time
spaces.purpose: Preserve timestep ordering across forward and reverse processes.

spaces.coordinate_system: abstract latent plane with depth for overlap separation
spaces.purpose: Show sample dispersion and convergence over time.
spaces.depth_semantics: Depth separates particles visually and is not a model dimension.

objects.renderer_notes: Do not imply it is a real dataset image.

objects.visual_form: timestep chain with beta_t and alpha_bar_t indicators
objects.renderer_notes: Keep timestep ordering exact even if curve values are schematic.

objects.visual_form: purple particle cloud around the sample
objects.renderer_notes: Use dispersion to show qualitative noising, not exact pixel statistics.

objects.visual_form: fading signal-strength curve with timestep slider
objects.renderer_notes: If a toy beta schedule is later supplied, this can be upgraded to numeric_exact.

objects.visual_form: reverse denoising trajectory with several timestep ticks
objects.renderer_notes: Show repeated steps rather than a one-shot jump.

objects.visual_form: forward transition, closed-form x_t, and reverse transition formulas
objects.renderer_notes: Never render readable math with AI video.

encodings.risk_note: Forward schedule and learned denoiser colors must not be swapped.
Cloud size is qualitative unless a toy schedule is declared.

layout_constraints.rule: Forward movement increases t; reverse movement decreases t.
Hold camera still when formulas and epsilon_theta labels are introduced.

beats.teaching_goal: Establish x_0 as the clean sample before diffusion starts.
beats.viewer_question: What state does the diffusion chain start from?

beats.teaching_goal: Show the fixed forward schedule adding noise over time.
Connect alpha_bar_t to direct sampling of x_t from x_0.
beats.viewer_question: Why can we sample x_t directly for any timestep?

beats.teaching_goal: Show learned reverse denoising as repeated steps.
beats.viewer_question: How does the learned model reverse the chain?

object_states.state: structured x_0 tile visible at t=0
cloud dispersion increases as t advances
selected x_t noise level appears for the slider position
epsilon_theta module receives x_t and timestep

transitions.technical_event: The fixed forward process adds Gaussian noise over timesteps.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: The noisy cloud represents the same sample identity after corruption.

transitions.timing_note: Diffuse cloud while the timeline advances.

transitions.technical_event: alpha_bar_t lets x_t be sampled directly from x_0.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: The same x_t abstraction is selected by the closed-form slider.

transitions.timing_note: Highlight formula first, then sweep slider.

transitions.technical_event: The learned reverse process denoises one timestep at a time.

object_correspondence.correspondence_note: Each reverse step should visibly preserve sample identity while reducing noise.

transitions.timing_note: Show two or three explicit steps before summarizing the remaining chain.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Show Gaussian noise being added to x_0 as t increases.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Viewer may think noising is learned.
Timeline direction must not reverse during forward noising.
Curve values are schematic without a declared toy schedule.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Select an x_t state directly from x_0 at a chosen timestep.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Direct sampling should not look like a separate learned model.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Feed x_t and timestep into the learned epsilon_theta module.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: Forward fixed schedule and learned reverse module must remain distinct.

motion_primitives.technical_meaning: Repeat reverse denoising steps from x_t toward x_0.
motion_primitives.misread_risk: A single convergence animation would imply one-step generation.

complexity_budget.notes: Use one process distinction per beat: clean state, fixed forward process, direct x_t sampling, learned reverse chain.

shots.hold_reason: x_0 and the timeline start need to be readable.
Track only along the timestep direction, then hold for formula.
The alpha_bar_t curve and formula must be compared.
Push in only after the repeated reverse-step rhythm is clear.
shots.depth_usage: Depth separates convergence particles, not exact latent dimensions.

camera_intents.intent: Camera motion must not invert forward and reverse timestep directions.
Hold on Gaussian formulas and epsilon_theta label before moving particles.

readability_constraints: Forward beta_t schedule must be marked fixed.
Particles are a teaching abstraction, not exact image pixels.
Do not show reverse denoising as a one-step jump.

exact_required.reason: Forward, closed-form, and reverse equations are source anchors.
Timestep ordering is central to the mechanism.
epsilon_theta must be labeled as the learned reverse component.

metaphor_allowed.allowed_scope: Particle clouds and convergence paths can communicate qualitative noising and denoising.
metaphor_allowed.boundary: They are not exact pixels, latent coordinates, or measured samples.

ai_video_forbidden: x_0, x_t, beta_t, alpha_bar_t, epsilon_theta labels
Claims that forward noising is learned

simplifications.statement: The particle cloud is a low-dimensional teaching abstraction.
simplifications.impact: It explains stochastic corruption and denoising order, not actual image pixels.
simplifications.disclosure_or_guardrail: Narration should call it an abstraction of x_t.

simplifications.statement: The beta_t and alpha_bar_t curves are schematic.
simplifications.impact: Schedule shape teaches monotonic signal/noise intuition without numeric commitment.
simplifications.disclosure_or_guardrail: Do not label exact numeric axis values unless a toy schedule is added.

responsibilities: Render Gaussian formulas, timestep labels, and schedule curves.
Render deterministic timeline and process labels.
candidate_renderers.reason: DDPM explanation depends on readable math and exact time ordering.
risks: Dense formulas can become unreadable if moved during particle animation.

responsibilities: Render particle cloud noising and reverse convergence in 2.5D.
Maintain stable sample identity across forward and reverse steps.
candidate_renderers.reason: Spatial particle motion makes noising and denoising easier to understand.
risks: Particles may be overread as exact pixels or latent dimensions.

responsibilities: Assemble formula overlays, particle clips, captions, and voice timing.
candidate_renderers.reason: The timeline spans several process phases.
risks: Layer desync could blur fixed-forward versus learned-reverse distinction.

responsibilities: Optional text-free atmospheric noise background only.
candidate_renderers.reason: Atmospheric support can be separated from exact formulas.
risks: May hallucinate fake text or image details.

fallbacks.tradeoff: Use 2D particle dots and schedule panels instead of 2.5D particle clouds.

storyboard_beat_refs.storyboard_note: Introduce x_0 and the start of the timestep chain.
Advance fixed beta_t schedule while the sample diffuses.
Use alpha_bar_t slider to select a direct x_t sample.
Route noisy state through learned epsilon_theta for repeated reverse steps.

enriched_scene_spec_hints.notes: Keep x_0 and the start of the timeline readable.
Forward noising is fixed schedule, not learned.
Closed-form direct x_t sampling should read as formula-driven.
Show repeated denoising, not one-step reconstruction.

downstream_tasks: Write storyboard.md for clean, forward, closed-form, and reverse beats.
Choose whether to include a toy beta schedule.
Generate deterministic formula overlays for forward and reverse transitions.

source_fidelity_checks: Forward noising is described as fixed, not learned.
Reverse denoising is described as learned and iterative.
The closed-form x_t sampling formula is separated from the reverse transition formula.
visual_readability_checks: Timestep direction remains consistent in forward and reverse scenes.
epsilon_theta label is deterministic and readable.
Gaussian formulas are rendered by deterministic layers.
misconception_checks: The reverse process is not shown as a one-step jump from noise to image.
Particle clouds are not described as exact image pixels.
beta_t schedule is not presented as learned.
renderer_acceptance_checks: Exact formulas and labels are absent from AI-generated support layers.
Formula panel holds during dense math.
Fallback 2D rendering still preserves time ordering and process distinction.

open_questions.question: Should this example define a toy beta schedule so the signal curve can become numeric_exact?

known_risks.risk: Viewers may treat the particle cloud as exact image pixels or latent coordinates.
known_risks.mitigation: Keep labels and narration explicit that it is a low-dimensional teaching abstraction.
